figures.caption: Fig. 1: Surround depth estimation for articulated vehicle.

TABLE II: Depth estimation on KITTI [20], DDAD [21], nuScenes [22] and our own self-collected datasets (Results on KITTI are scale-ambiguous evaluation. † indicates our modified GeoDepth or GeoSurDepth with proposed losses. # implies reproduced results by VFDepth [7]. * indicates modified framework for adaptation to articulated vehicle pattern. Results best in bold, second best underlined).

Fig. 2: Overview: (a) Network architecture of ArticuSurDepth; (b) Self-supervised training framework and its loss components: (Left) Within- and crossvehicle spatial context enrichment. Example: for the target view C5, the within-vehicle right view is C6, while the type-2 cross-vehicle right view is C0. (Right) Cross-view pseudo surface normal consistency (LPNC).

Fig. 3: Example of cross-vehicle extrinsics calibration: (a) LiDAR pointclouds registration; (b) Pointclouds of Lf projected on camera C6 (mounted on front vehicle); (c) Pointclouds of Lr projected on camera C6; (d) Withinand cross-vehicle spatial contexts and transformations.

Fig. 9: Visualization on val split: (a) Examples of depth and surface normal estimation on self-collected dataset; (b) Example of surround-view depth estimation on test split of self-collected dataset; (c) Examples of direct inference on DDAD; (d) Examples of direct inference on nuScenes.
Fig. 10: Example of ground plane detection or estimation, and camera height estimation (range of camera height estimation has been clipped for visualization).
